title: penstemon menziesii f. newberryi
intro: Penstemon menziesii f. newberryi, also known as Menzies' beardtongue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Penstemon menziesii f. newberryi include its ability to soothe sore throats and coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. Penstemon menziesii f. newberryi can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
